articles.title: WELO DATA: HUMAN JUDGMENT AS AI QUALITY INFRASTRUCTURE
articles.summary: Human judgment breaks down without systems, and AI quality follows. Welo Data [4] provides the scaffolding that both humans and AI need to practice robust evaluation at scale.

articles.title: Start applying human judgment at AI scale
OUR TEAMS HAVE BEEN BUILDING WITH A 2.5X-FASTER VERSION OF CLAUDE OPUS 4.6
articles.summary: Anthropic is making a faster version of Claude Opus 4.6 available as an early experiment via Claude Code and its API. Fast mode is more expensive to run, but it is 2.5x faster. It is designed for urgent, high-stakes projects. A link to the waitlist for the feature is available in the thread.

articles.title: META AI READIES AVOCADO, MANUS AGENT, AND OPENCLAW INTEGRATION
articles.summary: Meta AI is reportedly preparing to release new models named Avocado. It is also adding MCP support and a Memory section to its settings menu. Meta AI has revamped its website to include a lot of additional functionality. The company appears to be working on an AI agent and a browser agent, and a new feature called Tasks that will allow users to schedule recurring executions of Meta AI.

articles.title: NVIDIA BECOMES FIRST $5T COMPANY; AI CHIPS FUEL MARKET VALUE
articles.summary: Nvidia achieved a historic milestone by briefly surpassing a $5 trillion market valuation, driven by overwhelming demand for its AI chips and dominance in data-center accelerators. Its advanced Blackwell and Rubin GPU platforms power the bulk of large-model training and inference workloads, underpinning investor confidence in continued AI infrastructure growth. This valuation reflects Nvidia's central role in the AI economy and illustrates how hardware leadership translates to extraordinary market value.

articles.title: SELF-DRIVING CODEBASES WITH THOUSANDS OF AGENTS
articles.summary: Cursor scaled a system where thousands of agents collaboratively coded a functioning web browser with minimal human input, highlighting progress toward autonomous software development.

articles.title: OPEN MODELS WILL NEVER CATCH UP
articles.summary: Open models will probably never catch up with closed ones. However, they don't need to - open models are an engine for exploration in a way that companies can't really nurture. Open models are the main place where experimentation still happens. They will be the engine for the next ten years of AI research.

articles.title: THE LIMIT IN THE LOOP: MEMORY AS A SYSTEM PROBLEM
articles.summary: Weaviate discusses the limitations of current LLM applications rooted in session-based design, arguing that solving continuity—carrying context across interactions—requires systemic rather than model-level changes.

articles.title: WORLD MODELS AND THE DATA PROBLEM IN ROBOTICS
articles.summary: World models are trained to predict how the world evolves. They enable generalization that pure action prediction cannot achieve. Combining world models with robotics could create robots that can do everything humans can do. However, this will require a lot of data captured by real people.

articles.title: LEARNING FROM CONTEXT IS HARDER THAN WE THOUGHT
articles.summary: The role of humans in AI systems will shift if context learning improves significantly. Humans would focus on context engineering rather than primarily providing training data. Once this is achieved, we then need to focus on making context persistent.

articles.title: MONTY
articles.summary: Monty is a minimal, secure Python interpreter written in Rust for use by AI. It lets users safely run Python code written by agents. Monty completely blocks access to the host environment, and it can only call functions it has access to. It makes it possible to safely run LLM-generated code without the complexity of a sandbox or the risk of running code directly on the host.

articles.title: DFLASH SPEEDS UP SPECULATIVE DECODING
articles.summary: DFlash is a lightweight block diffusion model designed to accelerate speculative decoding in LLMs that achieves up to 6x speedup for Qwen3-8B.

articles.title: G'S LAST EXAM
articles.summary: The profession of software engineering has forever changed, but it is still unknown what role humans will exactly play. This post presents a list of the most ambitious and creative software accomplishments by humans. A world in which agents autonomously solve these challenges would be equal parts humbling, exciting, and unsettling.

articles.title: DO MARKETS BELIEVE IN TRANSFORMATIVE AI?
articles.summary: Transformative technologies influence interest rates by changing growth expectations, increasing uncertainty about growth, or raising concerns about existential risk. There were economically large and statistically significant movements concentrated at longer maturities for US bond yields around major AI model releases in 2023 and 2024. These movements correspond to downward revisions in expected consumption growth and/or a reduction in the perceived probability of extreme outcomes. It appears the markets do not believe in transformative AI.

articles.title: THE ANTHROPIC HIVE MIND
articles.summary: While every company quickly becomes professional and 'grown up', Anthropic still hasn't bothered. It isn't run like any other company of its size. The company's employees describe the company as a hive mind run entirely on vibes. This article takes a look inside one of the leading AI labs, examining the company's culture, history, work style, and more.

articles.title: EXPERTS HAVE WORLD MODELS. LLMS HAVE WORD MODELS
articles.summary: LLMs struggle in adversarial environments because they generate outputs based on static models rather than adaptive world models.

articles.title: LLMS COULD BE, BUT SHOULDN'T BE COMPILERS
articles.summary: Large language models can translate a specification into an executable artifact, but the control relinquished in that translation layer is significant.

articles.title: THOUGHTS ON CLAUDE'S CONSTITUTION
articles.summary: Claude's Constitution is the foundational framework from which Claude's character and values emerge.

articles.title: LORA BUT WITH ONLY 13 PARAMETERS??
articles.summary: Meta researchers say they can boost an LLM's math reasoning by updating just 13 parameters.

articles.title: SOFTWARE DEVELOPMENT IS UNDERGOING A RENAISSANCE IN FRONT OF OUR EYES
articles.summary: Adopting AI coding tools requires a deep cultural change that requires companies to figure out a lot of downstream implications.

articles.title: THE GENTLE OBSOLESCENCE
articles.summary: AI is becoming more and more capable, and this may make humans obsolete.
